item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image has been replaced with the following:

Filename: SCP-042-new.jpg
Name: White Horse Closeup
Author: Kim Newberg
License: Public Domain
Source Link: publicdomainpictures.net

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Following a read of comments - I don't really think it's as bad as some people say. It is pretty obvious the Foundation did not cut off its wings, otherwise it would have performed a far more invasive vivisection (leaving appropriate scars) if not an outright autopsy. It probably recovered the pegasus in this state and basically keeps it in store until someone has an idea what to do about the thing. I'm thinking of an equestrian-minded character (not a new SCP - every other noob wants to write a new SCP so I don't) who might have this SCP-427 idea, but I'd need a greenlight from the author here I guess.

I also don't really understand why it is Safe, especially following the latest incident in the document. It looks like a Euclid to me because it's sentient, possibly sapient, and can move on its own if it wants to. The only Safe sapient and mobile SCP I can remember is SCP-343, which probably altered its own records, but this SCP evidently can not alter its records.

(By the way if SCP-427 does not work, how about exposure to SCP-343? That carries a chance that SCP-343 might choose to terminate this SCP, though. Another SCP I'd try on this one is SCP-662, there is no termination risk there as SCP-662 follows orders, and SCP-662 might be able to feed the pegasus - though unlikely to heal it).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My theory would be that the Foundation might hold out some hope of getting the skip to a more useful state eventually, so it can be studied. However, this would mean actually trying to do so - and I just added a comment outlining an obvious method hat does not seem to involve much cost or much risk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a question. Shouldn't the Foundation expose this SCP to SCP-427? It's a chance to get an actual winged pegasus and study it! Giving it SCP-500 would probably be considered waste of he limited supply of SCP-500, but SCP-427 appears to be an infinite resource. Moreover, if the Foundation succeeds in healing the pegasus. the pegasus could well submit to research willingly.

SCP-427 is a healing, not wish-fulfilling, device, so I don't see a major danger of termination. Care needs to be taken to avoid overexposure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see where it says the Foundation cut off its wings. The Pegasus could have had its wings cut off prior to coming into Foundation custody.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't understand this article.

If the Foundation came in possession of Pegasus, would the inclination not be to study it? Not surgically remove it's one defining feature for ease of containment.

This just feels overly sadistic and more concentrated on portraying the Foundation as a heartless organisation. Personally, I don't like it.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image could not be sourced reliably and is thus assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is… pretty terrible. A sad pegasus that lost its wings and wants to die, but the foundation are just keeping it locked up and force feeding it. Not interesting and just paints the foundation as sadistic rather than a group interested in research and containment of useful or harmful entities. The creature seems neither useful or harmful and many scp entries demonstrate the foundation tends to avoid needless cruelty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking the same, there's a lot missing here that would normally be included in a skip. When/how was it discovered, when did containment start? Is there really no research done on why it dries out the ground? The info contained only seems to VERY loosely support the addendum events.

It really just mostly reads to me like someone making a SCP version of the the statement, "The Foundation is bad m'kay". Has potential but badly needs a clean-up/rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It tookme too long to realize it wss a downed pegasus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This…does not feel like an SCP entry…feels more like some…pathetic grimdark attempt.

I've read through a lot of SCPs, and what is going on here is just…my gods.

Someone tone down the grimdark, it does NOT work here in my personal opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like there is so much more that could have been done here. We don't know how the Foundation got this thing. We don't know why the Foundation hasn't killed it/let it die yet. We don't know what the hell it even does beyond draining water from the ground and attempting to get itself killed. There are no test logs, there are redactions in the containment procedures, and there is no history (or even mild implications) to be found. The article leaves all of those questions unanswered, giving us a bland SCP that really only serves to be a generic depressing article about the Pegasus that has no direction and does nothing but add to the grimdark. -1 until this thing gets a much needed rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, that one certainly didn't aged well

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's a depressed Pegasus who lost its wings?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted, and I doubt it will change.

There are several anomalous effects going on, and somehow the Foundation seems indifferent to any of them. On the other side, the horse is suffering and this seems to be all too important. The document dedicates itself more to describe how miserable its life is, and how the Foundation deepens this misery, but for no reason at all.

Answer first the basic questions. What this thing do? How was it found? What kind of test or observation could be done? Why is it so depressed? The miserable conditions this skip is in must have at least a hint to reason, even if an evil reason. Also, it certainly isn't Safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps I can explain my thinking from back when I rewrote this from an existing, poorly rated article. I wanted to convey the sense that the Foundation recognized that through their mismanagement, they had allowed a potentially irreplaceable asset to be rendered useless, likely permanently. Yet the Foundation's mission, and a certain greed for control, forbade them from simply discarding the broken remnant of this once majestic creature. Thus, the Foundation would rather subject it to any level of cruelty deemed necessary to retain the asset for potential future use. The cruelty here is not the goal, but simply an acceptable side effect of the mission, to which all other considerations are subordinate. They are not evil for evil's sake, but will commit any kind of atrocity for the sake of the mission. I welcome any refinements of the article which better convey this sense without straying into evil tropes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this was written in the early years. Since then, many articles have gone for the 'cold, but not cruel' route.

I suppose the authors of various recent articles prefer to do 'morally ambiguous yet justified', instead of 'just cruel for the sake of cruelty'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This interestingly follows my perspective; My perspective is that The Foundation is evil and secretly (Administrator eyes only) only exists as such to be cruel and unethical for convenient reasons. If The Foundation was in fact "good", they would place a bowl in the containment chamber containing a mixture of sugar and food designed specifically to attract the horse's desire of taste. Also to create strong bones, the horse should be allowed to be taken to a pasture and roam for multiple hours during the day. Due to this, Procedure-110 Montauk, and the GOI Hub of the "Chaos Insurgency", I am led to believe that The SCP Foundation is evil and does in fact NOT wish to "Secure, Contain, and Protect" but rather to Torture, Torture, and Kill; The Chaos Insurgency is in fact trying to stop The SCP Foundation for ethical reasons. Now, despite the other posts saying that the "Ethics Committee" should consider euthanasia, I do not consider it to be something more ethical. I almost downvoted because this seems to be mainly boring and describing a normal, neglected horse (other than the small bit that describes "bone wings"). +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: everyone is discussing about the cruelty of the situation and i'm just sitting here reminded by this scene from Robot Chicken

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with the sentiment that this currently feels too cruel. The animal isn't being studied and keeping it alive for no real purpose comes across as cruel, not cold. A little something needs to be added to justify the foundation behaviour, not much, just a hint that there's a reason it's being kept alive. A redaction in the right place, or another anomalous effect. Maybe a document that claims the creatures death would have catastrophic circumstances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it's been classed as Safe, and we don't gain anything from it still being alive/we won't lose anything if it dies, there's no reason to make the poor thing suffer. If it's for research purposes, that should be explicitly stated.

It's in the first 100, and the "beating a dead horse" pun is great, but come on, it feels more like we're reading about some sadistic animal abuser, not the SCP foundation. The Ethics Committee exists. The page badly needs editing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really agree with the sentiment that this seems to just make the Foundation sound cruel. I think in large part because the article is kind of disjointed, and almost seems more focused on how badly this creature is treated, rather than what makes it an SCP.

First, I think the 'drought' effect feels really tacked on. If there was a line about how the SCP speculates that this may be an intentional or subconscious antipathetic effect of whatever power it used on Dr. P, that would help that aspect feel less out of place.

From there, a bit of notation to suggest that they are trying to study the connection between the two effects would be in keeping with the theme of the wiki, and I think, better reinforce the we are 'cold not cruel' mantra of the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A once flying horse with the ability to force emotional states onto humans. That, combined with its ability to make someone fall into a coma means it needs containing. This is a foundation that also contains a love testing machine that can point out someone in the world who is compatible with the user.

SCPs don't have to be creepy or dangerous, simply anomalous enough to disrupt what people believe is 'normal' in our world if left uncontained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get that the horse is creepy, but what makes it special enough to be taken as an SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seriously. Is it searches including searching the food stuffs, or are we getting out alien anal probes?

It gives me feels, but I can't love it with that redaction. Nor without some idea why they aren't either breeding it, or letting it kill itself. At the least, why hasn't it been given another horse as a friend/mate? I know this isn't the hug everything society or something, but equines are social, maybe it'd be less a pain to maintain if it wasn't alone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted for the redactions in containment procedure. Not sure how I feel about it otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow that's sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Crash? I interpreted it as providing the horse the experience of flying (the weightlessness) once again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fine. Nolo contendere.

Any more, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The issue isn't with the existence of a joke, it's with the lack of content. You made a declamatory statement that didn't contribute anything to the conversation, and we all wasted time reading it. Now we're wasting time reading an entire conversation about it. Nobody was mad at a joke. People will get mad at the conversation. I strongly suggest you let it lie, without replying further. You will not benefit from continuing this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everyone's so mad at a joke.

I didn't realize that discussing pages on a horror wiki full of pseudo-scientific reports on made up objects was such serious business.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your comment would have been way better if you had just said that in the first place.

Skali's response was really inappropriate, yeah, but you'd be much more helpful by stating things like that instead of implying them with all-caps nonsense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well i was just pointing out that it was evulfoundation.
It was supposed to show the author that article needed some justification for all this abuse. Not incite rage from people who think the internet is a matter of life or death.

item.title: Holy crap
item.content:encoded: Remove the redactions in the containment procedure so I can upvote already.

item.title: Re: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: To clarify: That was an official warning. If you continue to break the rules, you risk further consequences.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: schifter, please refrain from making comments like this in the future. It's spammy and doesn't contribute any substance to the discussion.

item.title: Mod Post
item.content:encoded:
Skali Sharpnose, that was completely uncalled for. Please refrain from using unnecessarily vehement language in the future.

I'm calling a stop to this line of discussion.

As per site rules, don't reply to staff posts if you're not staff. If you have anything further to discuss, take it to PMs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Look you little inbred newbie piss-pot, if all you have to contribute is a generic meme caption you're parroting from ten year old 4chan slang, then you're in the wrong place. This is (or was) supposed to be a place for inventive, collaborative writing. If you have critiques, articulate them. If you have improvements, make them. The community will then absorb and build on them. But if all you're going to do is leer in caps lock like some kind of grinning half-wit, then keep it to yourself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: LOL EVUL FOUNAYSHUN!!1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same. Which is a shame, 'cause otherwise this is what I like to see in an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
10 seconds in, first reaction:
"What? Why is this redacted? What am I supposed to do to these people, terminate them or give them complimentary mudbaths?"
Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Frankly, considering it was once winged and now isn't, I'd really like to hear about how it came into Foundation custody and what the heck happened to its wings. The current article leaves a lot to be desired, and as others have said, redacting ANYTHING from the containment procedures is a big no-no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could I try tightening this up a little to get rid of the redactions in the containment procedures? I mean, how is anyone supposed to follow procedure if it's redacted?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted due to the redactions in the actual containment. If those weren't around, would have downvoted due to the sudden bout of horsegasm that doesn't seem to fit with the rest of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beat you to it by 1 minute

I hate horses. But this got to me. Poor thing.

:(
Also I made this fan art. http://i.imgur.com/DMPf3.png
(Here's what it's from it you don't get the reference.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one is so beautifully sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see why people say this one is a waste of resources, this thing takes pennies to contain compared to most SCP. That said, probably best to keep high level personal away from it due to its persuasive nature. Would be interesting to see if it could be cloned, or if a 38 cloned sample of 500 would have any effect on its health.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"We might need that emotion manipulating, maimed, wingless, suicidal horse in the future"
"For what?"
"We won't know until it happens, and if we needed the horse then we're really going to regret killing it off"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
what are we gaining from keeping this being in our captivity?
If we gain nothing from the study of this then why waste the money, its only causing this thing pain. We may pride ourselves as men of science, those who dont let things like emotions or religion hold us back from our study of the unknown, but in doing so we forget what it is to be human.

In short we are hurting a living being for no reason without anything outputting from it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, they could probably try to learn how any size wings could support a horse in the air if indeed it was previously able to fly.

And I've seen the Foundation people do some rather unethical things, but come on! It's a horse that lost its wings that is doing everything it can to die! Someone pointed this out earlier, but they are probably wasting resources on an animal that you really can't learn all that much from. I mean, you probably can't even approach it without a telekill helmet because it'll try to make you kill it! The only way to study the telepathy is to examine its brain. Which may mean poking around in it surgically if other means don't reveal anything.

Anything they could have learned from it should have been learned by now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The wonders of spray-paint …

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn't Famine's horse black? And why wouldn't it eat?

Maybe it's a mix of Famine's horse and the Pegasus?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Note also that the comment you are replying to is nearly 3 years old, and was posted to an older version of the article.

EDIT: Aaaand Reject beats me to the punch. Apologies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. It got rewritten since she left that comment.
  2. That comment was left over two and a half years ago
  3. This is a very good SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The entire point of the article is to display just what kind of people operate the Foundation, it's much easier to contain without it's pesky wings so why bother healing it? heck it could be useful in the future so we definitely can't end it's suffering.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How can you like this? They are torturing the poor suffering animal! It wants to die! Just let it! Or try to use other SCPs to regrow its wings!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And with that, I upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god.

It's not Pegasus.

It's Famine's horse.

No wonder it won't eat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Despite regular watering, SCP-042's presence leaves the ground parched wherever it treads."

I guess this makes it a drought horse?
